title: Rich Red-Cured Vietnamese Fish Pilaf
description: A dense and adventurous Vietnamese dish featuring a red wine vinegar and shio-koji cure, served in a banana leaf.
instructions: Combine shio-koji, red wine vinegar, fish sauce, and palm sugar in a bowl to make the cure.
Add the chopped lemongrass and sliced galangal to the cure and mix well.
Place the mackerel fillets in a shallow dish and pour the cure over them.
Refrigerate the mackerel for at least 2 hours or overnight.
Line a baking sheet with banana leaves and place the cured mackerel on top.
Bake the mackerel for 12-15 minutes or until cooked through.
While the mackerel is baking, toast the peanuts in a dry skillet over medium heat.
Chop the peanuts and set aside.
Once the mackerel is cooked, allow it to cool slightly before slicing it into pieces.
Serve the mackerel on a bed of toasted peanuts, garnished with chopped cilantro and lemongrass.
Fold the banana leaves over the mackerel to create a steamer basket and serve hot.
